content.text: Adoption metrics answer the question: is the Claude developer platform growing its consumer base, or is it plateauing? Growth metrics answer: are the teams that have adopted the platform using it more deeply over time, or are they churning? Both dimensions matter. A platform can have high initial adoption but low retention — teams that onboard and then revert to calling the Anthropic API directly — which indicates the platform is not delivering sustained value. Tracking adoption and growth metrics separately makes it possible to diagnose which problem to solve.

attrs.alt: Platform adoption funnel (Aware → Onboarded → Active → Champion) and four growth maturity stages

content.text: The adoption funnel has four stages. A team advances through the funnel as they go from discovering the platform to actively shipping Claude features to becoming an advocate who contributes back and recommends the platform to colleagues.
Team has visited the IDP or attended a platform demo
IDP page view for the team's account
# unique teams with ≥1 IDP visit in 30 days
Team has an API key and made a first successful Claude API call (M2)
Team has deployed at least one Claude feature in the last 30 days
claude.deploy.staging or claude.deploy.production event in last 30d
# teams with a deploy in the trailing 30 days
Team has ≥3 active Claude features AND is an NPS promoter (score 9–10)

The conversion rate between each funnel stage tells the platform team where friction is highest. Low Aware→Onboarded conversion signals discovery or first-impression problems. Low Onboarded→Active conversion signals that teams succeed with the initial experiment but struggle to scale it into production.

-- Adoption funnel conversion rates for the trailing 90 days
WITH funnel AS (
    SELECT
        team_id,
        MAX(CASE WHEN event_type = 'idp.page_view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)             AS aware,
        MAX(CASE WHEN event_type = 'claude.sdk.first_successful_call' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS onboarded,
        MAX(CASE WHEN event_type IN ('claude.deploy.staging','claude.deploy.production')
                  AND occurred_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS active,
        MAX(CASE WHEN event_type = 'nps.response' AND nps_score >= 9 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)  AS promoter
    FROM telemetry_events
    WHERE occurred_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days'
    GROUP BY team_id
),
counts AS (
    SELECT
        SUM(aware)     AS n_aware,
        SUM(onboarded) AS n_onboarded,
        SUM(active)    AS n_active,
        SUM(CASE WHEN active = 1 AND promoter = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_champion
    FROM funnel
)
SELECT
    n_aware,
    n_onboarded,
    ROUND(100.0 * n_onboarded / NULLIF(n_aware, 0), 1) AS aware_to_onboarded_pct,
    n_active,
    ROUND(100.0 * n_active / NULLIF(n_onboarded, 0), 1) AS onboarded_to_active_pct,
    n_champion,
    ROUND(100.0 * n_champion / NULLIF(n_active, 0), 1) AS active_to_champion_pct
FROM counts;

Growth metrics measure depth of adoption, not just breadth. A platform used by 30 teams each with one Claude integration is less mature — and more fragile — than a platform used by 20 teams each with an average of 4 integrations. The core growth metrics track consumption depth, SDK penetration, and retention.
Teams with ≥1 Claude deploy in the last 30 days
Average number of Claude features per active team
% of Claude-using services that import acme-claude-sdk
Total tokens consumed via platform across all teams
% of newly onboarded teams that are still active 30 days later
% of active teams that are NPS promoters

The platform team's investment priorities should match the platform's current growth stage. Investing in federation and multi-model routing while only 8 teams have adopted the platform is premature; failing to address retention and depth when 40 teams are active is a missed opportunity.

At the seed stage, the goal is to prove that the platform is worth having at all. The core investments are the SDK, the IDP with self-service provisioning, and the starter template. Success metric: 5 teams reach Active status within 6 months.

At the growth stage, the platform has proven its value and the goal is to accelerate adoption without proportional growth in the platform team's headcount. The core investments are golden paths (RAG, agents, observability baseline), the eval runner, and the inner-source contribution model. Success metric: TTFD p50 < 5 days; SDK adoption rate > 85%.

At the scale stage, the platform must enforce standards across a large and heterogeneous consumer base. The core investments are the versioning and deprecation policy, engineering scorecards, and architectural fitness functions. Success metric: 30-day retention > 70%; integrations per active team > 2.5.

At the mature stage, the platform is a load-bearing piece of the engineering organisation. The core investments are cost attribution and showback, multi-model routing for cost optimisation, and federated eval management across business units. Success metric: champion rate > 30%; platform NPS > 50.
The platform team reviews growth metrics every Monday alongside TTFD and SLA metrics. The growth dashboard is generated from the telemetry data warehouse and posted to #platform-devex.

# scripts/growth_dashboard.py  — weekly growth report
import pandas as pd
from slack_sdk import WebClient
import os

events = pd.read_parquet("data/telemetry_trailing_90d.parquet")

# Active teams (deploy in last 30 days)
deploy_events = events[
    events["event_type"].isin(["claude.deploy.staging", "claude.deploy.production"])
    & (events["occurred_at"] >= pd.Timestamp.now() - pd.Timedelta("30d"))
]
active_teams = deploy_events["team_id"].nunique()

# Onboarded teams (all time)
onboarded = events[events["event_type"] == "claude.sdk.first_successful_call"]["team_id"].nunique()

# SDK adoption rate
sdk_callers = events[events["event_type"] == "claude.sdk.first_successful_call"]["team_id"].unique()
direct_callers = events[
    (events["event_type"] == "anthropic.api.call")
    & (~events["team_id"].isin(sdk_callers))
]["team_id"].nunique()
sdk_adoption_rate = len(sdk_callers) / (len(sdk_callers) + direct_callers) * 100

# 30-day retention
onboarded_30d_ago = events[
    (events["event_type"] == "claude.sdk.first_successful_call")
    & (events["occurred_at"].between(
        pd.Timestamp.now() - pd.Timedelta("60d"),
        pd.Timestamp.now() - pd.Timedelta("30d")
    ))
]["team_id"].unique()
still_active = deploy_events[deploy_events["team_id"].isin(onboarded_30d_ago)]["team_id"].nunique()
retention_30d = (still_active / len(onboarded_30d_ago) * 100) if len(onboarded_30d_ago) > 0 else 0

msg = (
    f"*Platform Growth Dashboard*
"
    f"• Active teams (30d): {active_teams}
"
    f"• Onboarded teams (all time): {onboarded}
"
    f"• SDK adoption rate: {sdk_adoption_rate:.1f}% (target: ≥85%)
"
    f"• 30-day retention: {retention_30d:.1f}% (target: ≥70%)
"
)

client = WebClient(token=os.environ["SLACK_BOT_TOKEN"])
client.chat_postMessage(channel="#platform-devex", text=msg)

Churn is when an Active team stops deploying Claude features for 45+ consecutive days. A team that churns has either found a better solution, encountered an unresolved blocker, or had a business reason to deprioritise the Claude use case. Understanding which is critical: the first two are platform problems; the third is not.

-- Teams that churned in the last quarter (active in Q1, no deploy in Q2)
WITH q1_active AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT team_id
    FROM telemetry_events
    WHERE event_type IN ('claude.deploy.staging', 'claude.deploy.production')
      AND occurred_at BETWEEN '2025-01-01' AND '2025-03-31'
),
q2_active AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT team_id
    FROM telemetry_events
    WHERE event_type IN ('claude.deploy.staging', 'claude.deploy.production')
      AND occurred_at BETWEEN '2025-04-01' AND '2025-06-30'
)
SELECT
    q1.team_id,
    t.team_name,
    t.use_case,
    t.squad_size
FROM q1_active q1
LEFT JOIN q2_active q2 ON q1.team_id = q2.team_id
JOIN teams t ON q1.team_id = t.id
WHERE q2.team_id IS NULL
ORDER BY t.squad_size DESC;

Every churned team should receive a 15-minute exit interview within 30 days of their last deploy. The interview asks three questions: what were you building with Claude, what made you stop, and what would bring you back? Exit interviews consistently surface systemic issues that neither surveys nor telemetry can detect.

Engineering leadership typically cares about three questions: Is the platform growing? Is it sticky? And is it paying off? The quarterly platform report should answer these directly, with a single KPI for each.
Active teams this quarter vs. last quarter. Target: 15% QoQ growth.
30-day retention for newly onboarded teams. Target: ≥ 70%.
Claude feature velocity: average deploys per active team per quarter. A rising number indicates that the platform is enabling teams to ship Claude features faster, not just enabling the first one.

## Platform Adoption Report — Q2 2025

### Is it growing?
Active teams: 34 (Q2) vs. 27 (Q1) → +26% QoQ ✅
New teams onboarded: 12
TTFD p50: 3.1 days (down from 6.2 days in Q1) ✅

### Is it sticky?
30-day retention: 74% ✅
Churn this quarter: 3 teams (all for business reasons, not platform friction)
Integrations per active team: 2.8 (up from 2.1 in Q1) ✅

### Is it paying off?
Claude features deployed via platform: 187 (Q2) vs. 98 (Q1) → +91% ✅
SDK adoption rate: 91% (target: 85%) ✅
Platform NPS: 47 (up from 38 in Q1) ✅

### Next Quarter Targets
- Active teams: 40 (+18%)
- 30-day retention: 78%
- Integrations per active team: 3.2

Aggregate adoption numbers hide important patterns. A platform with 34 active teams that are all doing the same use case (customer-support summarisation) is more fragile than one with 34 teams spread across five use cases. Use-case segmentation reveals whether the platform is serving a diverse set of needs or a narrow one.

-- Adoption breakdown by use case (self-reported at onboarding)
SELECT
    use_case,
    COUNT(DISTINCT team_id)                                              AS active_teams,
    ROUND(AVG(integration_count), 1)                                    AS avg_integrations,
    ROUND(AVG(ttfd_staging_days), 1)                                    AS avg_ttfd_days,
    ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN is_sdk_user THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          / COUNT(*), 1)                                                 AS sdk_adoption_pct,
    ROUND(AVG(nps_score), 1)                                            AS avg_nps
FROM team_platform_summary
WHERE is_active = true
GROUP BY use_case
ORDER BY active_teams DESC;

-- Example output:
-- use_case               | active_teams | avg_int | ttfd | sdk% | nps
-- customer-support       | 11           | 3.2     | 2.8  | 94%  | 48
-- content-generation     | 8            | 2.9     | 3.1  | 88%  | 44
-- code-review            | 6            | 1.8     | 4.2  | 82%  | 39
-- data-extraction        | 5            | 2.1     | 3.5  | 91%  | 42
-- agent-workflows        | 4            | 1.2     | 7.3  | 71%  | 31

The agent-workflows segment in the example above shows concerning signals: high TTFD (7.3 days), low SDK adoption (71%), and low NPS (31). This is a platform investment signal — the golden path for agent workflows is underserving this segment, and the platform team should prioritise improving it.
Platform teams in SaaS companies track
— do existing customers spend more over time? The internal equivalent is
: do existing Claude-adopting teams ship more Claude features over time? Expansion depth is measured as the quarter-over-quarter change in integrations per active team.

# Expansion depth: QoQ change in integrations per active team
import pandas as pd

# Quarterly integration counts by team
q1 = {"team_a": 2, "team_b": 1, "team_c": 3, "team_d": 2}
q2 = {"team_a": 4, "team_b": 1, "team_c": 4, "team_d": 3, "team_e": 2}

# Only teams active in both quarters (expansion, not new adoption)
both_quarters = set(q1.keys()) & set(q2.keys())

q1_avg = sum(q1[t] for t in both_quarters) / len(both_quarters)
q2_avg = sum(q2[t] for t in both_quarters) / len(both_quarters)
expansion = (q2_avg - q1_avg) / q1_avg * 100

print(f"Cohort (both quarters): {len(both_quarters)} teams")
print(f"Q1 avg integrations (cohort): {q1_avg:.1f}")
print(f"Q2 avg integrations (cohort): {q2_avg:.1f}")
print(f"Expansion depth: +{expansion:.1f}%")
# Output:
# Cohort (both quarters): 4 teams
# Q1 avg integrations (cohort): 2.0
# Q2 avg integrations (cohort): 3.0
# Expansion depth: +50.0%

An expansion depth below 0% (teams shipping fewer Claude features over time) is a critical warning sign — it means the platform is not making Claude adoption easier to sustain, and teams are reducing their dependency rather than increasing it.
Engineering leadership eventually asks: what business outcomes does the platform drive? This is harder to measure than adoption metrics, but two proxies are consistently valuable:
(do teams ship Claude features faster than they would without the platform?) and
(do teams using the platform's eval gates have fewer production quality regressions?).
Compare against teams that built their own SDK wrappers (where data available)
Compare eval-gate users vs. non-users; track production NLU/quality incidents
50% fewer quality incidents for SDK users
Hours saved on Claude integration boilerplate
Engineering survey: estimate hours spent on auth, retry, telemetry vs. status quo
Time from model capability release to production adoption
Track time from Anthropic API changelog → first team using new feature via platform
a team that created an API key 6 months ago and never deployed is not an adoption win. Always measure on trailing 30-day deploy activity, not on sign-up counts.
Treating all use cases as equivalent:

a team with one Claude summarisation call per day contributes differently to platform health than a team with an agent workflow running 10,000 calls per hour. Consider token volume alongside team count.
reporting 'we added 12 new teams this quarter' without mentioning 'we also lost 5 teams' overstates platform health. Report net active teams, not gross additions.
Attributing business outcomes to the platform without controls:

if a team ships a successful Claude feature, some credit belongs to the platform (faster integration) and some to the team (good product thinking). Don't claim credit for outcomes the platform didn't drive.
Optimising the champion rate metric by pestering teams for NPS scores:
NPS should be collected once per quarter via the scheduled survey. Soliciting it more frequently to inflate the champion count destroys the signal.
Platform adoption and growth metrics answer whether the Claude developer platform is expanding its consumer base and delivering sustained value. The

(retention, integrations per team, SDK adoption rate) track depth. The platform's investment priorities should match its growth stage — seed, growth, scale, or mature. Weekly growth dashboards, quarterly churn analysis, and a three-question leadership report (growing? sticky? paying off?) keep the entire team oriented toward outcomes rather than output.
Track all four funnel stages separately; conversion rates between stages pinpoint where friction is highest.
30-day retention is the single most important growth metric — a platform that can't retain new adopters cannot scale.
SDK adoption rate ≥ 85% is the signal that the platform has won the 'build vs. use' decision for Claude integrations.
Growth stage determines investment priorities: friction removal (seed), golden paths (growth), standards (scale), optimisation (mature).
Exit-interview every churned team within 30 days — exit interviews surface problems that no other signal can detect.
Leadership report: three questions, three KPIs, one page. Growing? Sticky? Paying off?
